Catalog Description

Computer Systems Design. (1-6). Credit 3. Engineering design; working as a design-

team member, conceptual design methodology, design evaluations, total project

planning and management techniques, design optimization, systems manufacturing

costs considerations; emphasis placed upon students' activities as design

professionals. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in CSCE 315 or CSCE 331, and

CSCE 462 or ECEN 449, and ECEN 325; senior classification.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  Grade of C or better in CSCE 315 or CSCE 331, and

CSCE 462 or ECEN 449, and ECEN 325; senior classification.

Course Learning Outcomes

It is expected that successful participation in the course will allow the student to

demonstrate:

• an ability to apply knowledge of mathematics, science, and engineering (3.a)

• an ability to design and conduct experiments, as well as to analyze and interpret

data (3.b)

• an ability to design a system, component, or process to meet desired needs (3.c)

• an ability to function on multi-disciplinary teams (3.d)

• an ability to identify, formulate, and solve engineering problems (3.e)

• an understanding of professional and ethical responsibility (3.f)

• an ability to communicate effectively (3.g)

• an ability to use the techniques, skills, and modern engineering tools necessary for

engineering practice (3.k)

Moreover, course-specific outcomes are

1. understand and utilize the engineering design method to generate potential

solutions to a specific design challenge;

2. understand the basic of project management including: statements of work,

work break-down structures, critical path identification, distribution of tasks,

engineering practice (3.k)

Moreover, course-specific outcomes are

1. understand and utilize the engineering design method to generate potential

solutions to a specific design challenge;

2. understand the basic of project management including: statements of work,

work break-down structures, critical path identification, distribution of tasks,

project tracking and status reporting; be able to generate status reports;

3. understand professional communication methods and techniques including

formal reports and presentations;

4. clearly communicate ideas, status and test results through written reports;

5. clearly communicate through oral presentations.

6. to rigorously define design requirements for potential solutions.

7. understand and incorporate engineering standards and multiple realistic

constraints that include most of the following considerations: economic,

environmental, sustainability, manufacturability, ethical, health and safety,

social and political.

Special Course Designation

UCRT - University Required Oral Communication

C (communication-intensive course)

As a communication-intensive course, you will receive instruction on writing and

public speaking and complete assignments so you can practice communication

related to the major. You must pass the written and oral components with a C or

better to earn passing grade in the course.

Failure to pass the communication components with a C or better results in an F for

the course.

Textbook and/or Resource Materials

This material Is: Recommended

The Pentium Chronicles

Authors:  Robert P. Colwell

Publisher:  Wiley-IEEE Computer Society Press

Publication Date:  2006-01-01

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

• No late submission of reports, milestones or code from published dates.

• If there is a good reason, we need to talk during class hours. You need to

document and send email to myself and the TA. I will respond with an

explicit approval.

• If you or your team submits a milestone late without approval you will lose

the following % points from your awarded score:

• Late by no more than 3 days, 80% of max awarded score

• Late by 3+ days but no more than 1 week, 60% of max awarded

score

• Late by 1 week but no more than 2 weeks, 40% of max awarded

score

• Late by 2 weeks but no more than 3 weeks, 20% of max awarded

score

• Late by greater than 3 weeks, 0%

the following % points from your awarded score:

• Late by no more than 3 days, 80% of max awarded score

• Late by 3+ days but no more than 1 week, 60% of max awarded

score

• Late by 1 week but no more than 2 weeks, 40% of max awarded

score

• Late by 2 weeks but no more than 3 weeks, 20% of max awarded

score

• Late by greater than 3 weeks, 0%

• Final milestone and poster milestone cannot be late. Zero for delayed final

milestone and poster milestone submission.

AI Statement

•All detected plagiarism, cheating, and complicity will be reported to the Aggie Honor

System Office.

•The sanction is a zero on the assignment and a letter grade reduction for a single

infraction.

•The sanction can be a letter grade F or F* for a pattern of infractions.

•Academic dishonesty will not be tolerated. For assignments, such as status reports

or poster creation, the group is expected to write their material. Do not use AI

tools! Not even for polishing your report or correcting grammar. Use

spellcheckers and grammar checkers in document editors.

•Students should not have another person/entity do the writing of any portion

of any assignment for them, which includes hiring a person or a company to write

assignments and using artificial intelligence tools like ChatGPT, Gemini.

•Every student should understand that complicity – helping or attempting to help

another student commit an act of academic dishonesty – also constitutes

•Students should not have another person/entity do the writing of any portion

of any assignment for them, which includes hiring a person or a company to write

assignments and using artificial intelligence tools like ChatGPT, Gemini.

•Every student should understand that complicity – helping or attempting to help

another student commit an act of academic dishonesty – also constitutes

academic dishonesty and carries the same punishment as cheating and

plagiarism.

•Example if you have direct knowledge that another person on the class is

using AI tools for unapproved work, and you do not report it immediately,

you will be subject to the same sanction as the individual committing

plagiarism or unauthorized access of AI tools.

•Use of Chatgpt for coding: If you plan to use chatgpt to write any part of your code,

you need to discuss it with me, make clear and cogent arguments why this is the

best use of the team’s time, and we need to come to an agreement, this has to

be clearly documented.

•Use of Chatgpt for search and exploration is permitted. However, no code should be

generated. No reports should be generated. No cut-and-paste should be

employed. Any data should be retrieved from the original source.

•You should setup explicit guardrails (boundaries) that ensure there is “no creep”

i.e. gradual increase the footprint of chatgpt code as the project progresses,

beyond predefined boundaries.

•You need to email to me with all team members and get an approval from me (the

instructor). This cannot be done post “the act” of AI use. That will be treated as

an infraction.

•Example scenario: I will allow use of AI for a very limited number of use cases

where the code being generated is not fundamental or core to the project

success, but is ancillary or plays a supporting role. An example maybe

generating the use of a UI, where the core of the project is not UI. Again, this has

to be discussed and preapproved!

•When in doubt discuss with your TA or instructor.

•Always ensure proper, timely approvals of exceptions.

Course Specific Attendance Policy

Each student gets 3 free absences from attendance If you miss more than 3 class

room sessions and you do not have a medical excuse or critical personal excuse,

documented and approved, 1% of total score will be deducted per missed

absence above this limit.

Course Specific Makeup Work Policy

Since this is a team and project based course there is no makeup policy. For special

situations talk to the Instructor and get approved makeup assignments.
